stories.title: CPSC Recall — CreateOn Crayola Pip-Cubes Pulled for Magnet Ingestion Risk

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: CreateOn Crayola pip-Cubes (magnetic building sets) pulled from Michaels + Amazon.

Loose magnets can perforate intestines and cause death if swallowed.

Stop use now. Call 800-333-0549 for a free replacement.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Crayola pip-Cube sets are recalled — magnets can separate and be fatal if swallowed.

No injuries yet. Act now.

Free replacement: pipcuberecall@CreateOn.com or 800-333-0549

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you've got Crayola pip-Cubes at home, it's time to pull them out.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission and CreateOn have recalled about 9,400 Crayola-branded pip-Cube magnetic building sets due to a magnet ingestion hazard. The cubes — sold in sets of 24 or 27 in Bold Colors and Glitter varieties — were available at Michaels stores and on Amazon. The issue: the magnets inside can come loose when the seams of the cubes separate.

Here's why that matters: high-powered magnets are one of the most dangerous things a small child can swallow. When multiple magnets are ingested, they can attract to each other through the walls of the intestines, causing perforations, twisting, blockage, and in serious cases, blood poisoning and death. The CPSC has not received injury reports yet — but that's the window to act.

The good news: CreateOn is offering a free replacement set. Just stop using the recalled cubes now, take them away from your kids, and contact CreateOn:

📞 800-333-0549 (M–F, 9:30 AM–4 PM CT)
✉️ pipcuberecall@CreateOn.com
🔗 createon.com/recall/

No proof of purchase needed. Share this with anyone who has young kids — these were sold at major retailers and are likely in a lot of homes. Do you check recall notices regularly, or is this the first you're hearing about this one?
facebook.image_caption: RECALL: Crayola pip-Cube magnetic sets from Michaels + Amazon are recalled. Loose magnets = serious ingestion risk. Free replacement from CreateOn: 800-333-0549. 🚨

article.headline: CPSC Recall: CreateOn Crayola Pip-Cube Magnetic Sets Pulled — What COS Families Need to Know

stories.title: Pikes Peak Birding and Nature Festival — This Week in Colorado Springs (May 14–17)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
COS families: the Pikes Peak Birding and Nature Festival is this week (May 14–17). 🦅

73 field trips. Garden of the Gods. Paint Mines. Bear Creek.

Outdoor science for all ages.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The 11th Pikes Peak Birding Festival runs May 14–17 in Colorado Springs.

Field trips through Garden of the Gods, Paint Mines, Fountain Creek, and more.

A nature classroom for the whole family. 🌿

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Here's one coming up that's worth knowing about, COS families.

The 11th annual Pikes Peak Birding and Nature Festival runs May 14–17 right here in and around Colorado Springs. It's built around guided field trips through some of the most beautiful outdoor spaces in the region — Garden of the Gods City Park, Paint Mines Interpretive Park, Fountain Creek Nature Center, Bear Creek Nature Center, and more.

There are 73 field trips in total, along with 14 seminars, 5 workshops, and a "Birds, Brews and Bites" social for adults. The festival spans everything from prairie birding to mountain habitat — all within a short drive of Colorado Springs.

Most activities are covered by the registration fee, which is designed to be accessible. Pre-registration for the main draw closed April 26, so if you didn't sign up ahead of time, head over to pikespeakbirdingandnaturefestival.org to check whether any day-of options or free activities are available — some events at the Nature Centers are open to drop-ins.

Even if you're not a bird person, this is genuinely great outdoor science exposure for kids. The Pikes Peak region is one of the most biodiverse spots in Colorado and a day at Paint Mines is unforgettable. Have your kids ever been on a guided nature walk?
facebook.image_caption: Pikes Peak Birding and Nature Festival is this week — May 14–17 🦅. Garden of the Gods, Paint Mines, 73 field trips. Free and low-cost outdoor science for COS families.

article.headline: Pikes Peak Birding and Nature Festival Is This Week — What COS Families Should Know

stories.title: COS Downtown Farmers Market Opens Sunday — First Day at Acacia Park (Mother's Day Weekend)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
COS Farmers Market opens Sunday, May 10 at Acacia Park — 9 AM to 2 PM. Free admission, every Sunday through October. 🌽

Perfect Mother's Day morning with the family.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Sunday morning just got a little better, COS.

The Colorado Springs Downtown Farmers Market at Acacia Park opens for the 2026 season this Sunday, May 10 — and it runs every single Sunday through October 25. Hours are 9 AM to 2 PM. Admission is free.

Acacia Park is at 115 E Platte Ave in downtown Colorado Springs. You'll find local produce, artisan goods, and the makers that make this one of the city's best weekly family traditions. No reservations, no tickets, no stress — just show up.

And since opening day is Mother's Day, it's a genuinely easy plan: coffee, fresh berries, local honey, and a walk around downtown with the kids. Not a bad way to celebrate.

The market runs weekly through late October, so this is a habit worth building from day one of the season. We'll be checking in with market highlights throughout the summer. Any particular vendors or market finds you're hoping to see this year?
facebook.image_caption: COS Downtown Farmers Market opens Sunday, May 10 — opening day AND Mother's Day. Acacia Park, 9 AM–2 PM, free admission. 🌽 A weekly family tradition starts this weekend.

article.headline: COS Downtown Farmers Market Opens Sunday May 10 — Free Admission, Mother's Day Weekend

stories.title: D49 Student Success Center Opens for 2026–27 School Year — Two New ECE Classrooms

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
D49 families with young kids: the new Student Success Center opens in August 2026 with 2 new early childhood education classrooms.

If your child is approaching kindergarten age, visit d49.org for enrollment info.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D49 families with younger kids — this one's for you.

School District 49 is opening its new Student Success Center at the start of the 2026–27 school year in August, and it includes two brand-new early childhood education classrooms. That means more capacity for D49's youngest learners — the kids who are approaching school age and their families who are figuring out the transition into kindergarten.

Early childhood programs can be hard to navigate in Colorado Springs. D49 has been expanding its options in the northeast part of the city, and the Student Success Center represents a meaningful investment in that expansion.

If you have a child who will be approaching kindergarten age for the 2026–27 or 2027–28 school year, now is the time to visit d49.org and learn about enrollment timelines, eligibility, and what the early learning programs look like. These spots tend to fill quickly once enrollment opens.

What resources do you wish had been easier to find when your kids were getting ready for kindergarten?
facebook.image_caption: D49's new Student Success Center opens in August with 2 new early childhood education classrooms. If your youngest is approaching school age, visit d49.org now to learn about enrollment.

article.headline: D49 Opening New Student Success Center in August — Two New Early Childhood Education Classrooms

stories.title: COS End of School Year — Last Days, What to Plan for Early May

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
COS parents — May 22 is the last day of school for both D11 and D49.

That's 18 days away. Summer camps and library programs fill fast.

Now's the time to register. 

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
May 22 is coming faster than it feels like it is.

That's the last day of school for both D11 and D49 — and it's only 18 days away. For D11 families, graduation ceremonies run May 19–21 at Ed Robson Arena at Colorado College. D49 graduations are at World Arena on May 23.

But here's the thing about end of school: the families who have a summer plan on May 22 are the ones who started looking now. COS summer day camps, library programs, and parks programs fill up quickly — especially the good ones.

A few places to start right now:

🏕️ COS Parks & Recreation summer programs: head to cosparkfoundation.org
📚 Pikes Peak Library District summer reading program: ppld.org
🏊 YMCA of the Pikes Peak Region youth camps: ymcapikes.org

No pressure to have it all figured out — but a quick 20-minute search this week can save a lot of scrambling in June. What's your family's summer game plan looking like this year?
facebook.image_caption: D11 and D49 last day of school: May 22. That's 18 days. Summer camps fill fast — now's the time to look at COS Parks, the library reading program, and YMCA camps.

article.headline: COS End of School Year 2026: Last Days, Graduations, and How to Get Ahead on Summer
